items.q: Why was the Berlin Wall built in 1961?
items.correct: To stop the mass flow of East Germans to the West via Berlin (millions had fled), which was draining the GDR of skilled workers and threatening the regime's survival.
items.misconception: The Berlin Wall was built to stop a Western invasion of East Berlin.
items.random_wrong: The Berlin Wall was built by NATO to keep East Germans out of West Germany.
items.why: The Wall faced inward to trap GDR citizens, not outward against invasion. Students miss that it was an admission of weakness — people were voting with their feet.

items.correct: It failed to overthrow the Tsar but forced the October Manifesto creating the Duma, exposing autocracy's weaknesses and rehearsing the structures of 1917.
items.misconception: The 1905 Revolution achieved nothing because the Tsar stayed in power.
items.random_wrong: The 1905 Revolution was led by Lenin from the Winter Palace.
items.why: Lenin was in exile during 1905; the leadership was diverse. Trotsky called it 'the dress rehearsal' — failure in 1905 created conditions for 1917.

items.q: Did Gorbachev intend to dissolve the Soviet Union?
items.correct: No — he wanted to reform and save Soviet socialism; glasnost and perestroika unleashed forces (nationalism, economic chaos) he could not control.
items.misconception: Gorbachev took power planning to abolish communism and dissolve the USSR.
items.random_wrong: Gorbachev dissolved the USSR by signing a treaty with Ronald Reagan.
items.why: Gorbachev was a committed Communist reformer; the collapse was an unintended consequence. Students project the outcome backwards onto his intentions.

items.q: Which bacterium is responsible for plague?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Yersinia pestis. “Vibrio cholerae” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which English statute of 1351 sought to cap wages at pre-plague levels?
items.why: Correct answer: Statute of Labourers. “Statute of Westminster” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In which year did the Black Death first reach Sicily from the Crimea?
items.why: Exact answer: 1347. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
